2606.17838.title: Environment-Grounded Automated Prompt Optimization for LLM Game Agents
2606.17838.paper_summary: LLM agents in interactive environments are highly sensitive to their prompts, yet prompt engineering remains a manual, task-specific process. We introduce an automated prompt optimization framework for LLM agents that decomposes the observation-to-action pipeline into a goal-conditioned descriptor agent and an action selection agent, and iteratively refines each module's prompt through an LLM-driven evolutionary loop guided by environment returns. We propose a behavior analyzer to attribute episode outcomes to specific prompt components, and a mutator to propose targeted revisions to the prompt, before validating them through environment rollouts. We evaluate on all five BabyAI tasks in the BALROG benchmark, comparing our pipeline against BALROG's RobustCoTAgent under both plain and guided prompt initializations. Optimization improves performance consistently across tasks and conditions, without requiring updates to the model weights. On PutNext, a multi-step coordination task where the RobustCoTAgent achieves 0% success, our framework reaches up to 72.5% success rate using the same underlying LLM with optimized prompts. These results suggest that a multi-agent framework, combined with automatic prompt optimization, enhances LLMs without the need for fine-tuning or extensive human supervision.
2606.17838.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理在互動環境中對其提示非常敏感，但提示工程仍然是一個手動、特定任務的過程。<lb/>我們介紹了一個自動化的提示優化框架，該框架將觀察到行動的流程分解為一個目標條件描述代理和一個行動選擇代理，並通過環境回報引導的 LLM 驅動的進化循環迭代地優化每個模塊的提示。<lb/>我們提出了一個行為分析器，將情節結果歸因於特定的提示組件，以及一個變異器，提出針對性的提示修訂，然後通過環境回合進行驗證。<lb/>我們在 BALROG 基準的所有五個 BabyAI 任務上進行評估，將我們的流程與 BALROG 的 RobustCoTAgent 在普通和引導提示初始化下進行比較。<lb/>優化在各任務和條件下始終提高性能，而無需更新模型權重。<lb/>在 PutNext 這個多步協調任務中，RobustCoTAgent 的成功率為 0%，而我們的框架使用相同的基礎 LLM 和優化提示達到高達 72.5% 的成功率。<lb/>這些結果表明，結合自動提示優化的多代理框架在不需要微調或大量人類監督的情況下增強了 LLM。
2606.17838.author: Rean Clive Fernandes et.al.
2606.17838.authors: Rean Clive Fernandes, Lukas Fehring, Theresa Eimer, Marius Lindauer, Matthias Feurer
